reminder: Do not --force-recreate the prod stack; use your one deploy route instead. Manual recreate drifts and skips the version bump + changelog.

docker cp into a live container is NOT a deploy: it hot-patches, drifts, and is lost on the next rebuild. Deploy through the real pipeline. (Anchor the dev exclusion to the container-ref colon '-dev:' so a file path that merely contains 'dev' - e.g. ./dev/ or fix-dev-bug.js - cannot silence a real prod hot-patch.)
Do not skip the precommit hook (--no-verify / -n): fix the underlying issue instead of bypassing the gate.

Do not print or dump secret values. Check presence with grep -q / test -n / [ -n ], never cat/echo the value into the transcript, a log, or a file. (Tool-scope to Bash/PowerShell so this never fires on your own chat text that merely mentions a secret name.)
A helper for this already exists - reuse it instead of writing a new one (write less code, avoid divergence).
